title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCCXII. Jingles.
content:
[I do not know whether the following may have reference to the game of handy-dandy, mentioned in 'King Lear,' act iv, sc. 6, and in Florio's 'New World of Words,' 1611, p. 57.]

        Handy Spandy, Jack-a-dandy,
        Loved plum-cake and sugar-candy;
        He bought some at a grocer's shop,
        And out he came, hop, hop, hop.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Nursery Rhyme. CCCCXII. Jingles.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
